target.title: Revamp command authorization candidates
target.body:
## Summary

- replace the ad hoc POSIX authorization path with a Tree-sitter-backed command candidate planner built on `explainShellCommand`
- route shell/argv allowlist checks and allow-always persistence through planned candidates, including static shell-wrapper payloads
- convert exec control-shell policy to a generic static command matcher over normalized argv/options/operands
- keep approval storage compatible while preserving safer fallback behavior for dynamic wrappers, path-scoped payloads, and pipe-to-shell cases
- keep the UI showing the actual command while honoring `allowedDecisions`

## Decisions and rationale

- POSIX shell authorization now comes from one planned command model: `planShellAuthorization()` uses the Tree-sitter-backed `explainShellCommand()` path, and the plan is carried into allowlist evaluation, allow-always persistence, and shell rewrite. The old POSIX splitter/render fallback is no longer an alternate source of candidates.
- Inline shell candidates are generated once during policy evaluation and then reused. The node-host safe-bin rewrite no longer re-runs `evaluateSystemRunAllowlist()` on the rendered command; it verifies that the plan still belongs to the original shell payload and renders from that plan.
- Exec control-shell policy now follows `Tree-sitter static extraction -> normalized command invocation -> hard-coded policy rule`.
- `/approve` handling is removed from exec control-shell policy. `/approve` is a control/chat action, not an executable policy concept, and exec should not carry a special deny list for old agent behavior. The old command-analysis carrier fixture block that used `/approve` has been deleted instead of renamed to another binary.
- Safe bins are treated as narrow, argument-profiled execution shortcuts, not blanket shell trust. Static, parsed safe-bin candidates may be rewritten to resolved trusted paths. Dynamic shells, command substitution, line continuations, path-scoped wrapper payloads, and pipe-to-shell cases remain approval-gated unless the whole relevant command graph is allowed.
- Static reads under `.ssh` / `~/.ssh` through basic read commands are now approval-required hard-coded control policy. Dynamic paths are not resolved by this matcher; they remain governed by the normal exec approval flow.
- Background `&` is preserved as shell topology. The planner models it as a background relationship and the renderer emits `&` when rebuilding commands, instead of failing closed or collapsing it into a sequence separator.
- `deny` still wins when deny-mode policy is active. The authorization planner can explain candidates, but runtime policy decides whether execution is allowed.

## Behavior changes

- Static POSIX shell wrappers like `sh -c 'git status'` can now be authorized by the inner `git` candidate when the payload is static and safely parsed.
- Dynamic wrappers like `sh -c '$CMD'`, command substitution, line continuations, and path-scoped wrapper payloads stay approval-gated instead of becoming reusable inner approvals.
- `curl URL | sh` / `curl URL | bash` behavior is preserved: both sides must be allowed for auto-run; allow-always persists `curl` only; persisted `curl` alone does not authorize the shell side on rerun.
- Mixed allowlist + safe-bin shell-wrapper payloads are rebuilt before execution from the existing authorization plan so the executed wrapper uses trusted resolved paths.
- Exec control-shell policy denies OpenClaw channel login commands and requires approval for security audit suppression mutation through the same normalized command matcher.
- Static `cat` / `less` / `more` / `head` / `tail` reads under `.ssh` or `~/.ssh` now require explicit approval.
- `ask=always` approval requests no longer offer an unusable Always Allow action in the Control UI.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Candidate-based command authorization for POSIX shell and non-Windows argv execution, including shell-wrapper unwrapping, dynamic fallback, pipe-to-shell behavior, allow-always persistence, node-host execution, control-shell dangerous command policy, and UI approval decisions.

Real environment tested: Local macOS focused tests; local OpenClaw Control UI against the candidate worktree; Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/command-analysis/risks.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/command-explainer/extract.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/command-explainer/extract.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Focused local proof passed 15 files / 465 tests. `git diff --check` passed. Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ks1f6v0x35t56nymmxbaw4a1` / GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26135215597 completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0. Follow-up cleanup proof passed 2 files / 20 tests, then 6 files / 119 tests, then control-shell/preflight/carrier focused proof passed 4 files / 148 tests with 8 skipped. After removing the repo doc and deleting the old carrier fixture block, focused proof passed 4 files / 147 tests with 8 skipped. After the reduction pass, focused proof passed 7 files / 212 tests with 8 skipped, and `git diff --check` passed. After the test-rubbish audit cleanup, focused proof passed 5 files / 177 tests, `git diff --check` passed, and the changed-test diff is now +1804 / -944 / net +860.

Observed result after fix: Static shell wrappers can auto-run from trusted inner candidates; dynamic/path-scoped wrapper payloads still prompt; `curl | sh` requires both sides; allow-always saved `/usr/bin/git` for a live `sh -c 'git status --short'` run and the rerun produced no second approval request. Static `.ssh` reads through basic read commands now require approval.

What was not tested: Full release validation was not run. Windows behavior remains on the legacy fallback path. The local UI proof used `--no-channels`, so Slack/Discord listeners were not part of the manual proof.

## UI proof notes

- Started local OpenClaw from this worktree with `openclaw-local-test --repo ... --no-open --no-channels --ttl 2h`.
- The installed local-test helper was machine-locally adjusted to stop writing stale `tools.exec.mode = "auto"`; that was not a repo change.
- The Control UI displayed the actual command `sh -c 'git status --short'`, including Host, Agent, Session, CWD, Resolved, Security, and Ask fields.
- Clicking Always Allow resolved the prompt.
- A live chat/tool run then created a saved `/usr/bin/git` allow-always entry, rerunning the same prompt produced no second `exec.approval.requested` event within 30 seconds, and the temporary `/usr/bin/git` test entry was removed from `~/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json` afterward.

target.title: fix: resolve managed SecretRef provider auth
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92097.

This teaches provider auth resolution to bridge managed/non-env SecretRef provider `apiKey` entries through the active runtime config snapshot. The source config can keep the managed SecretRef marker/object, while runtime auth uses the already-resolved literal key from the matching runtime snapshot.

The change is intentionally narrow:

- only applies when the source provider entry has a managed non-env SecretRef marker/object
- only trusts a runtime snapshot when it matches the active source snapshot
- does not make marker-only source config work without a runtime snapshot
- keeps explicit provider api-key config ahead of ambient profile/env auth
- blocks plugin synthetic local auth from shadowing an explicit managed provider SecretRef

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — 64 passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, overall patch correct
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` — core and coreTests only

Live E2E/proof from Crabbox on this branch before the final synthetic-shadowing regression hardening:

- provider: `aws`
- lease: `cbx_4b1a7511118d`
- run: `run_57dd3b102653`
- positive SecretRef-backed custom provider reached fake upstream with Authorization present
- marker-only source config failed before upstream auth, preserving the intended invalid-config behavior

Known proof gap: `pnpm check:changed` delegated to Blacksmith Testbox but remained queued; I stopped our queued run `tbx_01ktvv2bwe5y5psshpae92fj2w` rather than leaving it pending.

target.title: Fix doctor preview channel SecretRef resolution
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix doctor preview to resolve configured channel SecretRefs before channel doctor checks so file/env-backed channel credentials do not abort read-only doctor runs.
- Preserve unresolved SecretRefs in doctor preview and handle typed unresolved SecretRef reads from channel doctor adapters as non-fatal degraded preview warnings.
- Keep default doctor exec safety intact by skipping gateway `secrets.resolve` when gateway credentials would require exec-backed SecretRefs without `--allow-exec`.

Fixes #91939.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts src/config/types.secrets.resolution.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean; no accepted/actionable findings)
- Crabbox AWS proof: `run_55cc85e094c9`, lease `cbx_30402b8de8bc`, slug `pearl-prawn`
  - File-backed Nextcloud Talk `botSecret` SecretRef: `doctor --non-interactive` exited 0, no unresolved SecretRef abort, no missing bot secret warning.
  - Exec-backed channel SecretRef plus exec-backed gateway auth without `--allow-exec`: `doctor --non-interactive` exited 0, exec provider did not run, gateway/local exec skip diagnostics present, unresolved channel preview handled non-fatally.

target.title: Fix disabled heartbeat one-shot cron retries
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91775.

- add an exact removable system-event enqueue handle for cron-delivered main-session system events
- retry disabled `wakeMode: "now"` one-shot main-session cron jobs with the existing bounded cron retry/backoff policy instead of disabling them immediately
- remove failed-attempt queued system events so retries do not leave orphaned prompt events, while preserving completion only for a heartbeat that actually runs and consumes the event
- include restart catch-up coverage for persisted skipped/disabled one-shot retry state

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - first pass found a stale `src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts` mock for the new system-events export
  - fixed the mock and re-ran clean with no accepted/actionable findings
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/infra/system-events.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Crabbox AWS live repro/proof:
  - provider: `aws`
  - run: `run_1c0ed22cf056`
  - lease: `cbx_883e31de8e01`
  - slug: `pearl-shrimp`
  - type: `c7a.8xlarge`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: disabled heartbeat no longer makes an immediate main-session one-shot cron job look terminal while leaving its queued system event behind.

Real environment tested: Crabbox Linux on AWS, provider `aws`, run `run_1c0ed22cf056`, lease `cbx_883e31de8e01`, slug `pearl-shrimp`, type `c7a.8xlarge`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the Crabbox script installed Node `v22.19.0` and pnpm `11.2.2`, created a detached `origin/main` worktree at `575cae59d4`, symlinked dependencies, then ran the same cron proof harness against `origin/main` and this branch with `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-91775-proof.mjs`.

Evidence after fix: `PROOF_SUMMARY` for the fixed branch reported `heartbeatCalls: 3`, `consumedTexts: ["hello"]`, `finalQueued: []`, two disabled retry snapshots with no queued events, and a final successful attempt where the one-shot no longer existed.

Observed result after fix: the first two disabled heartbeat attempts kept the one-shot enabled for bounded retry and removed each failed-attempt queued event; the third attempt consumed exactly one `"hello"` system event and deleted the completed one-shot.

What was not tested: a real Telegram/Discord channel round-trip was not needed for this fix because the bug and fix are in cron heartbeat state transition and in-memory system-event queue ownership; the live proof exercised the gateway-facing queue contract and real cron service path.

## Current-main regression proof

The same Crabbox run reproduced current `origin/main` behavior: `PROOF_SUMMARY` for base reported `enqueueReturnsRemovableHandle: false`, `heartbeatCalls: 1`, the job still existed with `enabled: false`, `lastStatus: "skipped"`, `lastError: "disabled"`, and `finalQueued` contained the queued `"hello"` event.

target.title: fix(openai): allow Codex Spark via harness
target.body:
## Summary
- allow `openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark` when the OpenAI provider is using the Codex harness transport
- keep Spark suppressed for direct OpenAI API / Responses routes where the API catalog rejects it
- add Spark to the Codex fallback catalog and update docs/tests

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark` was blocked globally as “not exposed by Catalog” even when the account’s Codex harness catalog exposes it.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux using a real signed-in OpenAI account with Codex harness access. Account identifiers are redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Queried the live Codex app-server catalog, then evaluated the current source manifest suppression behavior with the repo `extensions` directory as bundled plugin metadata.
- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ HOME=/home/clawuser node_modules/.bin/tsx -e '...listAllCodexAppServerModels({ agentDir: "[redacted-agent-dir]" })...'
Config warnings:
- plugins.entries.lossless-claw: plugin disabled (disabled in config) but config is present
{
  "found": true,
  "model": {
    "id": "gpt-5.3-codex-spark",
    "hidden": false,
    "inputModalities": ["text"],
    "supportedReasoningEfforts": ["low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"],
    "defaultReasoningEffort": "high"
  }
}

$ HOME=/home/clawuser OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=/home/clawuser/openclaw/extensions OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 node_modules/.bin/tsx -e '...resolveManifestBuiltInModelSuppression(...)...'
{
  "codexHarness": null,
  "directResponses": {
    "suppress": true,
    "errorMessage": "Unknown model: openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark. gpt-5.3-codex-spark is not exposed by the OpenAI API catalog. Use the Codex harness route when your signed-in account exposes it."
  },
  "directApiHost": {
    "suppress": true,
    "errorMessage": "Unknown model: openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark. gpt-5.3-codex-spark is not exposed by the OpenAI API catalog. Use the Codex harness route when your signed-in account exposes it."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The live Codex harness catalog reports Spark as available and non-hidden for the signed-in account; OpenClaw’s current source suppression rules no longer suppress the Codex harness route (`codexHarness: null`) while direct Responses/API-host routes still suppress Spark.
- **What was not tested**: Did not restart the production gateway or run a full end-to-end assistant turn on Spark from the installed built CLI.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/provider.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/config/config.model-ref-validation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.forward-compat.errors-and-overrides.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-suppression.test.ts src/model-catalog/manifest-planner.test.ts`

Note: full `tsc --noEmit` OOMed at 2GB and 4GB; an 8GB run stayed CPU-active for ~16 minutes with no output and was stopped. A direct `pnpm tsgo:prod` attempt later got stuck in pnpm dependency status/install retrying optional package fetches, so I ran the underlying core tsgo command directly.

target.title: test(sqlite): add state perf query plan harness
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a dedicated SQLite state benchmark harness that seeds real OpenClaw shared and per-agent SQLite databases, runs integrity checks, measures hot queries, checkpoints WAL files, writes JSON artifacts, and prints stable `SQLITE_PERF_*` proof lines.
- Adds package scripts for smoke, default, and large SQLite perf runs.
- Adds query-plan regression coverage for shared state and per-agent cache hot paths so index drift fails in Vitest instead of relying on benchmark timing.
- Intentionally out of scope: broader SQLite resilience, backup/restore inventory, and unrelated gateway E2E failures from the SQLite plan execution.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91616

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-requested local BUG-005 extraction from the SQLite validation plan.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Missing SQLite-specific perf benchmark scripts and missing query-plan test coverage for hot shared/per-agent SQLite state paths.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux source checkout on branch `bug-005-sqlite-perf-query-plan`, Node `v24.15.0` runtime available in the workspace, with repo dependencies resolved through the existing checkout install.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `node --import tsx scripts/bench-sqlite-state.ts --profile smoke --output .artifacts/sqlite-perf/smoke.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/bench-sqlite-state.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```shell
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts
 ✓ |unit| src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts > sqlite hot query plans > uses shared state indexes for list and queue queries 121ms
 ✓ |unit| src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts > sqlite hot query plans > uses per-agent cache indexes for session metadata and expiry scans 41ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.10s

SQLITE_PERF_PROFILE=smoke
SQLITE_PERF_STATE_ROWS=4100
SQLITE_PERF_AGENT_ROWS=1000
SQLITE_PERF_INTEGRITY=ok
SQLITE_PERF_WAL_BYTES_BEFORE=3979952
SQLITE_PERF_WAL_BYTES_AFTER=0
SQLITE_PERF_QUERY_P95_MS=0.204
SQLITE_PERF_ARTIFACT=.artifacts/sqlite-perf/smoke.json

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.82)
```

- Observed result after fix: The query-plan test passes and the smoke benchmark creates representative SQLite state, verifies integrity, checkpoints WAL to zero bytes, emits a JSON artifact, and prints stable proof lines.
- What was not tested: The default and large SQLite perf profiles were not run locally; no full `pnpm check`, full `pnpm test`, Docker, cross-OS, or live provider lane was run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is a local harness/test coverage fix. The smoke run proves the new harness path and output contract; large-volume performance baselines should run in scheduled or release validation environments.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```shell
MISSING test:sqlite:perf:smoke
MISSING test:sqlite:perf
MISSING test:sqlite:perf:large
MISSING scripts/bench-sqlite-state.ts
MISSING src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node --import tsx scripts/bench-sqlite-state.ts --profile smoke --output .artifacts/sqlite-perf/smoke.json`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/bench-sqlite-state.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `src/state/sqlite-query-plan.test.ts` for SQLite query-plan index selection across cron jobs, cron run logs, delivery queue, plugin state, channel ingress, and per-agent cache queries.
- Added `scripts/bench-sqlite-state.ts` plus `test:sqlite:perf:*` package scripts for repeatable SQLite load/perf proof.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The SQLite plan inventory found the harness, package scripts, and query-plan test were missing.

If no test was added, why not?

- A focused query-plan test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

Benchmark maintenance risk: the seed SQL must stay aligned with the committed SQLite schemas.

How is that risk mitigated?

The benchmark opens the real OpenClaw shared/per-agent DB schemas and the smoke script was executed successfully. Query-plan assertions are in Vitest so index drift is caught by focused test proof.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and any maintainer-requested large-profile or remote performance proof.

target.title: fix(update): preserve local package overrides
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves local package overrides during package update flows and reports the recovery bundle when overrides are not reapplied.
- Keeps content-inventory validation strict for new tarballs while allowing already-published `2026.6.5` / `2026.6.5-beta.6` packages that predate `dist/postinstall-content-inventory.json`.
- Repairs the `main` merge conflict on `src/cli/update-cli.ts` and `src/infra/update-runner.ts`.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD && git diff --check`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/update.md scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.mjs scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.ts scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.mjs src/cli/update-cli.ts src/cli/update-cli/progress.ts src/cli/update-cli/shared.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/infra/package-dist-inventory.test.ts src/infra/package-dist-inventory.ts src/infra/package-local-overrides.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.ts src/infra/update-runner.ts test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts`
- `TMPDIR=/tmp node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts src/infra/package-dist-inventory.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/update-runner.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` clean after two accepted findings were fixed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Package self-update preserves locally modified installed package files by default, reapplies trusted overrides only when requested, reports conflicts when upstream changed the same file, and still exposes the preserved recovery bundle when an in-place update command fails after partially mutating the install. The PR also keeps published `2026.6.5` and `2026.6.5-beta.6` tarballs valid even though those already-shipped packages do not contain the new content-inventory file.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on current PR head `fdd50d1aa245744840d171e66ecc4ca76e6c22e2`, Node `v24.14.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`. The smoke used real filesystem temp package roots and the real `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps` / `writePackageDistInventory` implementation through `node --import tsx`; it was not a Vitest/mock assertion path. Earlier Blacksmith Testbox smoke remains supplemental for the original branch behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a current-head terminal smoke with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`, creating temporary installed package roots, writing real package dist inventories, mutating installed `dist/index.js` as a local override, applying staged update contents, and invoking `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps` for default preserve, trusted reapply, conflict preserve, and in-place failure recovery scenarios. Then ran focused tests, formatter check, `git diff --check`, and nested autoreview on the same head.

Evidence after fix:

```text
CRABBOX_PHASE:local-current-head-smoke
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE default-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"preserved","modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export {};","recovery":"export const local_default_preserve = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE trusted-reapply {"failedStep":null,"status":"applied","modified":1,"applied":1,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export const local_trusted_reapply = true;","recovery":"export const local_trusted_reapply = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE conflict-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"conflict","modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[{"path":"dist/index.js","reason":"target-changed"}],"installed":"export const upstream = true;","recovery":"export const local_conflict_preserve = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE in-place-failure-recovery {"failedStep":"global update","status":"preserved","modified":1,"applied":0,"installed":"export const partial_update = true;","recovery":"export const local_failed_update = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE passed
```

Supplemental current-head proof:

```text
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  269 passed (269)
```

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.78)
```

Observed result after fix: Default update preserved one local override and left the updated installed file in place while recording the local contents in the recovery bundle. Trusted reapply restored the local override into the installed package. Conflict handling kept the upstream-updated file, recorded a `target-changed` conflict, and preserved the local contents for recovery. The in-place failure path reported `failedStep: "global update"` and still emitted `local overrides` status `preserved` with the original local file content in the recovery bundle. Focused tests also covered the content-inventory compatibility cutoff and the in-place failure recovery reporting added during review.

What was not tested: A full `pnpm check` / full repository Vitest run was not rerun after the conflict repair because the touched surface was covered by the focused update, tarball, postinstall, inventory, CLI option/progress tests, formatter, diff check, current-head real filesystem smoke, and nested autoreview. A fresh remote Crabbox run was not repeated after the merge-conflict repair; the current-head real smoke above is the after-fix behavior proof for this branch.

target.title: Fix voice-call trusted proxy matching for IPv4-mapped webhooks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86525.

Normalizes voice-call trusted proxy IP comparisons before webhook URL reconstruction decides whether forwarded host/proto headers are trusted. This aligns Twilio/Plivo webhook signature verification with the existing media-stream client-IP path when Node reports a trusted IPv4 proxy as an IPv4-mapped IPv6 address.

## Motivation / root cause

`reconstructWebhookUrl` compared `trustedProxyIPs` to the socket remote address with a literal string match. A config value of `127.0.0.1` therefore did not match `::ffff:127.0.0.1`, so valid Twilio/Plivo callbacks behind a loopback proxy ignored `X-Forwarded-Host` and reconstructed `https://localhost/...` instead of the provider-signed public URL.

## User-visible behavior

Voice-call webhooks behind local or loopback reverse proxies continue to verify provider signatures when Node reports the proxy peer in IPv4-mapped form. Forwarded headers are still ignored unless `allowedHosts` is configured or `trustForwardingHeaders` is explicitly enabled, and the request still has to come from a trusted proxy when `trustedProxyIPs` is set.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: valid Twilio/Plivo webhook signatures were rejected when the request remote address was `::ffff:127.0.0.1` but `trustedProxyIPs` contained `127.0.0.1`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node v24.10.0 with pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script calling `verifyTwilioWebhook` with `remoteAddress: "::ffff:127.0.0.1"`, `trustedProxyIPs: ["127.0.0.1"]`, `x-forwarded-host: proxy.example.com`, and a valid Twilio signature for `https://proxy.example.com/voice/webhook`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "verificationUrl": "https://proxy.example.com/voice/webhook",
  "isReplay": false,
  "verifiedRequestKey": "twilio:req:56cf1e50f664e1464033f16c08ad96ce0a96b29597f4be9126eb8f1d0029412a"
}
```

Observed result after fix: the verifier trusts the forwarded public URL only for the normalized trusted proxy and accepts the valid signature.

What was not tested: no live Twilio, Plivo, or reverse-proxy E2E was run; this is local runtime proof plus focused verifier tests.

## Regression tests

Added focused tests covering both provider signature paths:

- Twilio signed webhook with `remoteAddress: "::ffff:127.0.0.1"` and `trustedProxyIPs: ["127.0.0.1"]`.
- Plivo V3 signed webhook with the same trusted proxy normalization shape.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-voice-call.config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook-security.test.ts` - 26 tests passed.
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` - lanes: `extensions`, `extensionTests`.
- `pnpm check:changed` - passed.
- `git diff --check -- extensions/voice-call/src/webhook-security.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook-security.test.ts` - passed.
- Claude Review for Codex, model `opus 4.7` - approved; no material findings.
- Codex code review - no blocking issues.
- Codex Security diff review - no actionable security findings.

## Compatibility and risks

Compatibility: this keeps existing exact trusted proxy config working and only canonicalizes equivalent IPv4-mapped/plain IPv4 peer representations. It does not add CIDR support or change the documented forwarding-header opt-in requirements.

Risk: low. The changed condition is security-sensitive, but forwarded headers are still gated by the same `allowedHosts` / `trustForwardingHeaders` requirement and the normalized socket peer match.

target.title: security: scan markdown skill definitions for injection threats
target.body:
## Summary

The skill scanner (`src/security/skill-scanner.ts`) previously only scanned JavaScript/TypeScript-style source files for threats. This PR extends it to also scan Markdown `.md` files, including `SKILL.md` definitions, for injection and obfuscation patterns.

Markdown-specific rules added:
- `hidden-unicode` detects zero-width characters and text-direction overrides that can hide malicious content from visual review.
- `markdown-data-uri` detects data URIs with executable MIME types such as `text/html` and `application/javascript`.
- `markdown-download-exec` detects `curl`/`wget` download-and-execute pipelines, including common bypass forms like `sudo bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, line continuations, and quoted pipe characters inside arguments.
- `markdown-encoded-payload` detects large base64 blocks inside Markdown.
- `markdown-hex-payload` detects hex-encoded payloads inside Markdown.

Implementation notes:
- Added `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` and `isMarkdown()`.
- Added `.md` to `SCANNABLE_EXTENSIONS`.
- Applies Markdown-specific rules only to Markdown files, while preserving code-specific scanning behavior for code files.
- Uses the raw Markdown source for Markdown source-wide rules so URLs like `https://...` are not accidentally truncated by code-comment stripping.
- Adds focused tests for Markdown findings, Markdown/code rule separation, directory scanning, and summary counts.

Why this matters: skill definitions can include rendered Markdown. Without scanning these files, hidden instructions, obfuscated payloads, or download-and-execute commands in skill Markdown can bypass the existing code scanner.

Supersedes #10705 (auto-closed by stale bot). Rebased cleanly onto current `main`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm check:changed`
- [x] Direct scanner smoke for Markdown download-and-execute bypass forms plus benign Markdown prose and table negative cases

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Markdown skill definitions such as `SKILL.md` are now scanned for hidden Unicode, executable data URIs, download-and-execute pipelines, and encoded payloads that previously fell outside the JavaScript/TypeScript scanner surface.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS at `/Users/alexalaniz/Code/openclaw-43469`, based on current `openclaw/openclaw:main` (`24bd0b212f`), with dependencies installed using `pnpm@10.33.2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the actual scanner through `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs` against Markdown sources containing `curl`/`wget` bypass forms, then ran `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, and `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
markdown scanner smoke passed 7 positives and 2 negatives

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  32 passed (32)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.08s

All matched files use the correct format.

Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

Observed result after fix: The actual parser-based scanner caught 7 Markdown download-and-execute bypass samples in the smoke run (`sudo bash`, `sudo -E bash`, `env FOO=1 bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, a backslash-newline before `| bash`, and a quoted pipe inside a curl header) and did not flag benign curl prose or `| curl | bash |` Markdown table cells, while the targeted scanner test file and changed-file gate completed successfully.

What was not tested: No known gaps.

target.title: Node run/install: add --header for WebSocket upgrade (Cloudflare Zero Trust)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw node run` and `node install` had no way to pass custom HTTP headers on the WebSocket upgrade request, causing silent 302 redirects and connection failures when the node gateway sits behind Cloudflare Zero Trust or similar auth proxies
- **Why it matters:** Teams deploying OpenClaw in Zero Trust network environments (common in enterprise) were completely blocked — the WS handshake would redirect instead of upgrading, with no workaround
- **What changed:** Added a repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` CLI flag to both `node run` and `node install`; headers are persisted to config and forwarded to the gateway WebSocket client; also supports `OPENCLAW_NODE_HEADERS`, `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, and `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` env vars for the installed daemon service
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to gateway routing logic, auth token handling, skill execution, or any behavior for users not passing `--header`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42792
- Related: none

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw node run` now accepts repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` flags
- `openclaw node install` now accepts repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` flags; headers are written to config and used by the installed daemon on startup
- New env vars supported: `OPENCLAW_NODE_HEADERS` (JSON), `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET`
- No behavior change for users who do not pass `--header`

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `Yes` — headers may contain auth tokens; persisted to existing node config file (same location/permissions as all other config)
- New/changed network calls? `Yes` — user-supplied headers forwarded on outbound WebSocket upgrade request
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- **Risk + mitigation:** Headers containing secrets are written to the existing node config file (not a new location). No headers are logged or surfaced in output. The `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` env var name makes sensitivity clear to users.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.x (Apple Silicon)
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22, pnpm monorepo
- Model/provider: N/A (gateway connectivity issue, not model-specific)
- Integration/channel: Cloudflare Zero Trust (simulated via mock HTTP proxy)
- Relevant config: none required

### Steps

**1. Start a mock proxy that returns 302 if the CF header is absent:**
```js
const http = require('http');
const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {
  if (!req.headers['cf-access-client-id']) {
    res.writeHead(302, { Location: 'https://example.cloudflareaccess.com' });
    res.end();
    return;
  }
  res.writeHead(200);
  res.end();
});
server.listen(9999, () => console.log('Mock proxy listening on 9999'));
```

**2. Without fix — run in second terminal:**
```bash
pnpm openclaw node run --host localhost --port 9999
```

**3. With fix — run with headers:**
```bash
pnpm openclaw node run --host localhost --port 9999 \
  --header "CF-Access-Client-Id: test123" \
  --header "CF-Access-Client-Secret: secret456"
```

### Expected

- WS upgrade request includes supplied headers
- No 302 redirect when correct Zero Trust headers are present

### Actual (before fix)

- WS upgrade sent with no custom headers → Cloudflare returns 302 → `Unexpected server response: 302`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

**Before fix:**
```
Unexpected server response: 302
```

**After fix:**
```
Unexpected server response: 200
```
_(200 is expected — mock is not a real WS server; the point is the 302 redirect is gone)_

New unit tests added:
- `src/gateway/client.test.ts` — headers passed to WS constructor
- `src/daemon/program-args.test.ts` — `--header` args emitted correctly
- `src/cli/node-cli/header-args.test.ts` — parse logic including edge cases

All 895 test files, 7301 tests passing. `pnpm check` clean.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - `node run` with single `--header` → header present in WS upgrade
  - `node run` with multiple `--header` flags → all headers forwarded
  - `node run` with no `--header` → behavior identical to pre-fix
  - Mock proxy 302→200 transition confirmed end-to-end
  - `pnpm check` and `pnpm test` both clean on branch
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Malformed header string (missing `:`) → `parseHeaderArgs` throws with clear error
  - Header value containing `:` (e.g. URLs) → splits on first colon only, value preserved
  - Empty header value → accepted (valid HTTP)
- **What I did not verify:**
  - Live Cloudflare Zero Trust environment (tested via mock proxy only)
  - `node install` daemon persistence across system restarts
  - Windows path behavior for config persistence

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — `--header` is purely additive; existing behavior unchanged
- Config/env changes? `Yes` — new optional `headers` field in node config; old configs without it continue to work
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert: remove `--header` flags from invocation, or delete `headers` key from node config file
- Files/config to restore: `~/.openclaw/node-config.json` — remove the `headers` field
- Known bad symptoms: WS connections failing after upgrade if headers are malformed; `parseHeaderArgs` throws at startup with a descriptive error

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** User accidentally persists a sensitive token to disk via `node install --header`
  - **Mitigation:** Consistent with how other sensitive config (API keys) is already handled in node config. No new attack surface; file permissions unchanged.
- **Risk:** Header injection via malformed `--header` value
  - **Mitigation:** `parseHeaderArgs` validates format; `ws` library handles header serialization — no raw string concatenation into HTTP stream.

target.title: fix(gateway): surface OAuth auth URLs in wizard protocol
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: RPC wizard clients could ask users to paste an OAuth redirect URL without receiving the authorization URL in the wizard protocol.
- Why it matters: Windows/remote wizard clients cannot reliably complete OAuth setup when the URL only appears in gateway stdout or logs.
- What changed: Added optional structured `step.auth` metadata for wizard text steps and wired OAuth redirect URLs through shared provider flows, OpenAI Codex, Chutes, and Gemini.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): API-key/token paste flows and existing device-code note flows remain unchanged; CLI logging/browser-open behavior is preserved.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: OAuth redirect auth flows now include the authorization URL in `wizard.start` / `wizard.next` text-step metadata via optional `step.auth`, so protocol clients can show an Open/Copy URL affordance next to the redirect paste prompt.
Real environment tested: Windows development worktree for OpenClaw gateway/protocol/provider code on Node/pnpm; no live Windows companion app or real provider OAuth credentials were used.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src\gateway\protocol\wizard.schema.test.ts src\plugins\provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts src\plugins\provider-oauth-flow.test.ts src\commands\auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test && pnpm lint:core && pnpm lint:extensions`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: Focused protocol/provider tests assert `step.auth.url`, `step.auth.kind`, and provider metadata for shared OAuth flows, plus direct coverage that RPC wizard prompters force protocol-visible manual auth presentation.
Observed result after fix: Focused Vitest run passed 41 tests / 5 files; typecheck and lint lanes passed; `git diff --check` passed.
What was not tested: Manual Windows companion app end-to-end OAuth flow, real provider OAuth browser login, and full repository `pnpm check` / `pnpm test`.
Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Prior behavior only emitted OAuth URLs through `runtime.log` / gateway stdout while the protocol step only asked for a redirect URL.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: OAuth redirect flows logged authorization URLs to runtime output but did not carry the URL in the wizard step returned to RPC clients.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered local CLI/provider OAuth behavior but not protocol-visible auth metadata for RPC wizard clients.
- Contributing context (if known): Gateway local/remote detection is insufficient for RPC clients such as Windows/WSL, where the client needs to render the auth challenge even if the gateway environment appears local.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/protocol/wizard.schema.test.ts`, `src/plugins/provider-oauth-flow.test.ts`, `src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts`, `src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`, `src/wizard/session.test.ts`, `extensions/google/oauth.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: OAuth redirect text steps include structured `auth` metadata, shared provider handlers include provider ids when known, unknown auth schema fields/kinds are rejected, and RPC wizard prompters present auth challenges manually.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the protocol schema, wizard prompter seam, shared OAuth helper, and representative provider integrations without requiring live OAuth credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A; new focused coverage was added.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

RPC wizard clients can now render OAuth authorization URLs from `step.auth.url` on text steps instead of relying on gateway stdout/logs. Terminal CLI users still see the same logged URLs and prompts.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
OAuth provider -> gateway runtime.log(url) -> RPC client only sees "paste redirect URL"

After:
OAuth provider -> wizard text step auth.url -> RPC client shows auth URL + paste box -> user completes login
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A. The authorization URL was already logged for users; this change carries it through structured protocol metadata. Redirect URLs containing authorization codes remain user input, not `step.auth` metadata.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows
- Runtime/container: Node/pnpm OpenClaw worktree
- Model/provider: OpenAI Codex OAuth, Chutes OAuth, Google Gemini CLI OAuth seams via focused tests
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway wizard RPC protocol
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Start an RPC wizard-backed provider OAuth login flow that uses redirect-URL paste.
2. Observe the returned text step.
3. Confirm the step includes `auth.kind = "oauth-redirect"` and `auth.url` with provider metadata when known.

### Expected

- The protocol-visible text step carries structured auth metadata for clients to render.

### Actual

- Focused tests confirm `auth` metadata is attached for shared OAuth handlers, Codex, Chutes, and Gemini seams.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Protocol schema accepts structured auth metadata and rejects unknown auth fields/kinds; shared OAuth helper carries URL/kind/provider; Codex and Chutes pass provider ids; RPC wizard prompters force protocol-visible auth presentation; Gemini still forwards auth URL metadata.
- Edge cases checked: Provider field remains optional and omitted when unknown; local manual fallback reuses the last auth URL; CLI prompter remains unaffected because it ignores unknown text params.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Windows companion app OAuth UX, live browser OAuth with real credentials, full repository test suite.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Protocol clients might accidentally depend on `auth.provider` being present.
  - Mitigation: `provider` remains optional in schema/type and tests cover omission when unknown; clients should key rendering from `auth.kind` and `auth.url`.
- Risk: OAuth URLs become visible in an additional protocol field.
  - Mitigation: These URLs were already intentionally printed to runtime logs for user action and do not contain the returned authorization code; secret-bearing redirect input is not placed in `step.auth`.

AI-assisted: Yes. Implemented with Copilot-assisted sessions and reviewed by a separate adversarial Opus 4.8 session before PR creation.

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish Workboard card layout, timestamps, density controls, detail drawer behavior, mobile modal/dropdown behavior, and tooltip handling
- add health summary badge highlighting, filter-aware views, stable card action slots, card-derived agent filter options, and better empty states
- add guarded Workboard auto-refresh with dispatch-safe refresh sequencing, stale refresh protection, and disconnect cleanup
- fix Workboard lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes

## Change breakdown

### Workboard controls and refresh behavior
- Stabilized the toolbar so refresh state, auto-refresh, dispatch, density toggle, archive toggle, and last-updated time do not jump around during refreshes.
- Made the last-updated display a stable timestamp instead of switching between transient refresh text and time text.
- Hid the manual Refresh button while auto-refresh is enabled to reduce repeated visual state changes.
- Kept Dispatch ready work available during refresh when the action itself is still valid.
- Added guarded refresh sequencing so stale refresh responses do not overwrite newer Workboard state.

### Card layout and details
- Added per-card updated timestamps with local browser-time rendering.
- Reworked card headers so actions, timestamps, and chips use stable positions across density modes.
- Made card action slots consistent so buttons do not jump as density or content changes.
- Changed compact timestamp copy to use the clock icon plus date/time instead of truncating `Updated: ...`.
- Updated the details drawer to show matching date and time precision.
- Made clicking the already-selected card close the details drawer.

### Health metrics and highlighting
- Added the Workboard health metric bar for running, blocked, stale, ready unassigned, missing proof, and failed attempts.
- Made metric badges interactive: clicking a metric highlights matching cards.
- Matched highlight colors to the selected metric instead of always using red.
- Highlighted the active metric badge so the current metric selection is visible.
- Blended selected-card side color into the highlighted border so the card treatment looks intentional.
- Made health counts derive from live card/task/session state instead of manual labels.

### Filtering and dropdowns
- Replaced inconsistent native/custom filter dropdown behavior with a shared Workboard select control.
- Made selecting one dropdown close the previously open dropdown.
- Capitalized priority labels for consistency.
- Disabled zero-result view preset options where appropriate and added an empty-state message for no-result filters.
- Made status/view filters hide zero-result columns so filtered views are useful.
- Limited single-column filtered layouts on desktop instead of stretching one column across the full screen.
- Improved agent filter/assignment options by deduping configured agents, default/unassigned agent handling, and card-assignee-derived agents.

### Tooltips and icons
- Replaced unclear density icons with clearer list/dense-layout icons.
- Added tooltips across Workboard icon buttons.
- Reduced tooltip delay globally for Workboard buttons.
- Restored the compact one-line tooltip style and fixed tooltip clipping/overlap issues.
- Suppressed hover tooltips on mobile detail interactions where they obscure the fullscreen panel.

### Mobile and responsive behavior
- Fixed mobile horizontal overflow so cards fit within the board container without x-scroll.
- Restored card visibility after the mobile column/layout adjustment.
- Made the new-card modal fullscreen on mobile and larger on desktop.
- Fixed mobile dropdown placement inside the new-card modal.
- Changed modal dropdown menus to overlay instead of consuming layout real estate.
- Made mobile details open as a fullscreen panel.

### New card and demo data flows
- Improved the new-card modal layout to avoid nested vertical scroll problems.
- Added Workboard CLI/demo proof data for live boards, including seeded cards across statuses except Running.
- Added and duplicated demo card batches for the requested agents/boards during validation.

### Tests, i18n, and CI
- Added/updated Workboard controller, view, tooltip, lifecycle, settings, and E2E coverage.
- Regenerated Control UI i18n outputs for the new Workboard strings.
- Fixed CI lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes.
- Updated PR proof with live Workboard CLI output so the real-behavior proof gate passes.

## Verification
- `git merge --no-ff upstream/main` - clean merge; refreshed the PR branch to head `1ff6db89ff22628bc7657bb13b80e1fa39a1617e` on current `upstream/main` `575cae59d4864d9e215ee66d80d26f4e345021b5`.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.test.ts` - 2 agent test files, 85 tests passed; 5 UI test files, 183 tests passed.
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Workboard operations view layout, filters, health badges, tooltip-safe card actions, custom dropdowns, edit/create modals, drag/reload flow, read-only behavior, guarded auto-refresh, diagnostics refresh on writable tab load, and the stale-base CI failures after the Phase 1 polish branch fell behind `main`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR branch at head `1ff6db89ff22628bc7657bb13b80e1fa39a1617e`, after a clean merge from `upstream/main` `575cae59d4864d9e215ee66d80d26f4e345021b5`, using the real local Workboard plugin state plus focused deterministic Workboard/UI and previously failing agent-core test files.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
pnpm openclaw --version
pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.test.ts
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:test:ui
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI on current head `1ff6db8`:
```text
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (1ff6db8)

$ pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
{
  "enabledCount": 80,
  "workboard": {
    "id": "workboard",
    "enabled": true
  }
}

$ pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
{
  "cardCount": 10,
  "statuses": {
    "triage": 1,
    "backlog": 1,
    "todo": 1,
    "scheduled": 1,
    "ready": 3,
    "review": 1,
    "blocked": 1,
    "done": 1
  },
  "readyByAgent": {
    "main": 2,
    "gadget": 1
  }
}
```

Linked visual proof from the Chromium Workboard E2E artifact set:
- Create dialog screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/3oczzf.png
- Edited card screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/4b90q2.png
- Lifecycle review screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/anspcb.png
- Read-only board screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/21bnki.png
- Drag/status persistence screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/ggu4pd.png
- Writable flow recording: https://files.catbox.moe/1asjze.webm

Observed result after fix: The live Workboard plugin is enabled and the real Workboard CLI lists cards across expected non-running statuses, including three Ready cards for dispatch/view validation. The local CLI also reported a config-version mismatch and plugin index migration warnings for the existing local state; those warnings did not block Workboard plugin discovery or card listing. The two CI failures from the stale base were covered locally by rerunning `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts` and `src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.test.ts` after merging current `main`, and both now pass. Focused Workboard tests, UI typecheck, i18n check, diff whitespace check, and branch autoreview all pass on the pushed head.

What was not tested: A live production Gateway with private user data was not dumped in the PR; live proof summarizes plugin/card state without printing card contents or secrets. Full CI is rerunning on pushed head `1ff6db89ff22628bc7657bb13b80e1fa39a1617e`.

target.title: Expose paged channel action results

## Summary

- Preserve the existing Discord `thread-list` raw `threads` result while adding normalized pagination metadata at the action boundary.
- Surface `complete`, `hasMore`, `returnedCount`, `source`, `query`, and `nextBefore` for archived thread pages when Discord reports `has_more`.
- Cover the archived-thread `has_more` regression and the missing archive timestamp fallback in the Discord action runtime tests.

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high`
- Crabbox/Testbox E2E on the earlier PR head: provider `<test-provider>`, lease `<test-lease>`, Actions run `<actions-run-url>`; checked out `<pr-ref>` and ran `corepack pnpm test:docker:npm-onboard-discord-channel-agent`, result `npm tarball onboard/channel/agent Docker E2E passed (discord)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord thread-list actions no longer hide Discord pagination under the raw provider payload; agents now get explicit completion and cursor metadata while existing callers can still read the prior raw `threads` field.

Real environment tested: A locally configured Discord bot in a test guild `<guild-id>`, using read-only OpenClaw message actions with the local bundled Discord plugin override so the patched source plugin was exercised.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 pnpm openclaw message thread list --channel discord --guild-id <guild-id> --channel-id <channel-id> --include-archived --limit 2 --json`

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the local Discord `<channel-name>` archived-thread command:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "items": [],
  "complete": true,
  "hasMore": false,
  "returnedCount": 0,
  "source": "discord.threadList.archived",
  "query": {
    "guildId": "<guild-id>",
    "channelId": "<channel-id>",
    "includeArchived": true,
    "limit": 2
  },
  "threads": {
    "threads": [],
    "members": [],
    "has_more": false
  },
  "members": []
}
```

Observed result after fix: The active guild thread-list and archived `<channel-name>` thread-list both completed successfully through the Discord plugin and produced the normalized contract shape. A read-only sweep of local text channels found no archived threads, so the live result proves the compatible empty-page path without creating or uploading any Discord content.

What was not tested: A live non-empty archived-thread page with `has_more: true`, because the local Discord server had no archived threads in the swept text channels and I did not create or post test data. The paged cursor path is covered by focused regression coverage in this PR.
